title: Stallioneer v0.1.2
content:
Stallioneer has moved into a much more practical cataloging pass. This release focused on making day-to-day entry faster, giving the inventory view stronger management tools, and letting one title carry richer information across both the book record and its individual physical copies.

What changed:

  • Faster intake flow: ISBN and code entry now centers on immediate lookup results, speed-mode cataloging, manual fallbacks, and a smoother scan-first workflow that reduces how often book details have to be filled in by hand.
  • Stronger inventory controls: Inventory now supports better sorting and filtering, bulk selection and delete actions, collection assignment, cleaner title-first navigation, and copy/apply improvements that make existing records easier to update in place.
  • Richer record detail: The edit experience now separates common and extended fields more clearly, supports flexible additional-info rows, and adds GitHub-style live tags for both books and individual copies so collection notes can stay specific without slowing down entry.

This version keeps Stallioneer pointed at real shelf work: quick enough for repeated intake, structured enough for later cleanup, and detailed enough to distinguish one copy from another when the catalog gets larger.
